data_schema: id(int row id, keep verbatim for submission); breath_id(int, groups 80 consecutive timesteps of ONE breath — this is the sequence/group key); R(int lung resistance), C(int lung compliance) — constant within a breath; time_step(float seconds); u_in(float inspiratory valve control 0..100); u_out(int 0/1 exploratory valve, 1=expiratory phase); pressure(float target, cmH2O, train only).
extra_notes: Kaggle Ventilator Pressure Prediction. Time-series regression: predict airway pressure per timestep. train=6.03M rows in 75450 breaths of 80 steps each. LOCAL CV_SCORE = MAE (LOWER better). IMPORTANT: the OFFICIAL leaderboard metric is MAE computed ONLY on inspiratory timesteps (u_out==0); compute local CV both ways but report cv_score as the u_out==0 MAE to match official. Group-aware CV: split by breath_id (GroupKFold) so no breath leaks across folds. Strong features: within each breath_id sort by time_step then lag/lead u_in, cumulative sum of u_in, time since breath start, R*C. lightgbm/xgboost work; for speed you MAY subsample breaths for CV but predict all test rows. submission.csv columns EXACTLY id,pressure for all 4024000 test rows in order. metrics.json {"cv_score": <mae float>, "metric": "mae"}.
